title: gitlab-pipeline-stats config
description: Configuration file for the gitlab-pipeline-stats CLI: a list of pipeline groups (server-side and/or client-side filters) to compute per-job stats over.

$schema.description: URI of the JSON schema used to validate this file (informational, ignored at runtime).

description.description: Human-readable summary of the config (e.g. release flow it models). Shown nowhere by the CLI; useful in `list-presets` and editors.

groups.description: Pipeline groups to fetch and aggregate. Each group becomes a separate section in the output.

GroupSpec.description: One pipeline group. Server-side filters (ref/scope/source/status) are passed to GitLab's /pipelines API; client-side filters (refPattern/excludeRef/excludeRefPattern) are applied to the response.

label.description: Group label printed as the section header (=== <label> ===).

ref.description: Exact ref to filter by (e.g. "main", "develop"). Server-side: passed to GitLab as ?ref=.

scope.description: Pipeline scope (server-side, ?scope=). Note: "branches" returns only branch-ref pipelines; merge-request pipelines (refs/merge-requests/N/head) are NOT included — use source: merge_request_event for those.

source.description: Pipeline source (server-side, ?source=). Use merge_request_event to capture MR pipelines, which are missed by scope=branches.

status.description: Pipeline status (server-side, ?status=). For job-level status filtering use the CLI flag --status-filter.

refPattern.description: Client-side regex applied to pipeline.ref. Pipelines whose ref does NOT match are dropped after fetch.

excludeRef.description: Client-side: drop pipelines whose ref equals this value exactly.

excludeRefPattern.description: Client-side regex applied to pipeline.ref. Pipelines whose ref MATCHES are dropped after fetch.
